text: Last class you found the trip that changed you.
Today you learn to tell it so people feel every moment.
Jaw-dropping
Awe-inspiring
Beyond words
To take your breath away
Unforgettable
Once in a lifetime
Spellbinding
Remarkable
Sheer
To marvel at
To stop you in your tracks
Second to none
Never have I seen anything so beautiful.
Not only was the view stunning, but the silence was too.
Rarely do you find a place like this.
Little did I know it would change everything.
So, Marcelo, tell me. Never have I forgotten that glacier lagoon. What is the one place that stopped you in your tracks?
For me, it was a mountain road in Patagonia. Never have I seen a sky so wide. It was jaw-dropping.
That sounds awe-inspiring. Was it just the view?
Not only was the view amazing, but the silence was spellbinding. Little did I know I would remember it for years.
Rarely do two travelers share a story that good. For scenery, Patagonia is second to none.
It truly is. Some places are simply beyond words. That is why the story is worth telling.
Not only was the view unforgettable, but the silence was spellbinding too.
Little did I know it would become a once in a lifetime memory.
Not only was it huge, but it was spellbinding too.
Little did I know it would stay with me forever.
Rarely do you find a place so remarkable.
It was beyond words, truly second to none.

Hi Marcelo, it is Ronja. You asked me about the most jaw-dropping place I have ever seen, so here is my story. Two years ago I hiked to a glacier lagoon in the far north. Never have I seen anything so beautiful in my life. The water was a sheer, impossible blue, and huge pieces of ice floated past like slow ships. Not only was the view unforgettable, but the silence was spellbinding too. It literally stopped me in my tracks. I stood there for an hour, and honestly, the feeling was beyond words. Little did I know that a simple walk would become a once in a lifetime memory. Rarely do I call a place remarkable, but that one truly was. You have to go one day. Talk soon.

Welcome back to the show. Today, one question: why do some stories stop you in your tracks, while others we forget in a minute? Here is the secret. It is not only what happened, but how the person tells it. Great storytellers use emphasis. They do not say the view was nice. They say, never have I seen anything so beautiful. They build the moment. They pause before the surprise, and then they say, little did I know how much it would change me. Rarely do we remember a flat list of facts. But a story told with feeling, with a jaw-dropping detail and a spellbinding pause, sticks in the mind for years. So the next time you share an experience, do not just report it. Mark the moment. Make your listener feel the sheer wonder you felt. That is the story worth telling.

First, Ronja sets the scene and says she hiked to a glacier lagoon in the far north. Then she gives the big reaction: never has she seen anything so beautiful. After that, she describes the sheer blue water and the spellbinding silence. Next, she adds the twist: little did she know it would become a once in a lifetime memory. Finally, she calls the place truly remarkable and tells Marcelo he has to go.
